page: Amazon Web Services c5.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8275CL x86_64 processor with 24 physical cores and 48 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz. The instance is equipped with 96.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Benchmark results place the c5.12xlarge in the average performance tier across multiple workloads. The Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512, which assists in vector and cryptography operations. In CPU benchmarks, it achieves a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 47304.54 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 12291.0. Memory performance shows a read-only cached small block bandwidth of 2620.77 GB/sec, while uncached large block read and write bandwidths reach 117.04 GB/sec and 49.12 GB/sec, respectively.

This instance is designed for compute-intensive workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. It performs well in software compilation, with a Geekbench Clang compilation score of 33293.0, and static web serving, achieving a throughput of 27.07 GB/sec. However, LLM text generation performance is limited, with the small 135M model reaching 70.26 tokens/sec and the medium 7B model reaching 10.91 tokens/sec. The c5.12xlarge is best suited for high-compute tasks such as batch processing, distributed analytics, and web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services c5.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and an Intel Xeon 8275CL x86_64 processor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs operating at 3.6 GHz, paired with 96.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 47304.54 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 12291.0. It supports AVX-512 instructions, enhancing vector processing and cryptography tasks. Given its 1:2 RAM-to-vCPU ratio, the c5.12xlarge is highly cost-efficient for compute-heavy workloads that require high clock speeds but moderate memory footprints. It is well-suited for software compilation, batch processing, and static web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs, 96 GB DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8275CL processor, designed for compute-heavy workloads without local storage.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c5.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 48 vCPUs, 96 GB DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor running at 3.6 GHz.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual server featuring forty-eight dedicated vCPUs and ninety-six gigabytes of memory for intensive processing workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8275CL processor with 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores
96.0 GB of DDR4 memory at 3 GHz with 2.0 GB per core
12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor
Supports AVX-512 vector and cryptography instruction sets
No local storage or GPU hardware included
Optimized for software compilation, batch processing, and web serving
